articles.title: Some in MAHA movement express frustration over Supreme Court ruling on Roundup weed killer
articles.description: One member of the “Make America Healthy Again” movement said the decision was "sickening" after the Supreme Court ruled that Bayer, the manufacturer of Roundup, did not need to warn consumers of a potential cancer risk associated with its weed killer. NBC News’ Julie Tsirkin reports on the MAHA movement's frustration with the Supreme Court's ruling.

articles.title: Insurance increasingly dropping weight loss drugs like Wegovy, Zepbound
articles.description: More and more employees are losing insurance coverage for weight loss drugs like Wegovy and Zepbound, which may change where customers go to get the drugs.

articles.title: Exercise, not liraglutide alone, drives vascular gains after obesity-related weight loss
articles.description: A prespecified secondary analysis of the S-LiTE trial found that regular exercise reduced carotid artery wall thickness and circulating levels of inflammatory cytokines in adults with obesity who had already achieved substantial diet-induced weight loss. Liraglutide alone did not significantly improve these vascular or inflammatory markers, while exercise plus liraglutide also improved endothelial function biomarkers.

articles.title: Scientists find a universal gut microbiome pattern linked to colorectal cancer
articles.description: Researchers analyzed 6,779 stool microbiome profiles and 906 tissue samples to identify a colorectal cancer microbial signature that remained consistent across age groups, populations, and sequencing methods. The signature aligned with tumor-resident microbes, showed weaker detection for adenomas, and was inversely associated with dietary fiber intake.

articles.title: Beating the winter blues: Prevent and treat seasonal depression
articles.description: Psychologist Sandy Rae shares tips on how to avoid the winter blues, also known as 'SAD'.

articles.title: New Zealand's secret weapon that gives them the edge over England in the extreme heat - and why both sets of players are suffering behind the scenes
articles.description: RICHARD GIBSON: The weather that greeted the start of the 68th such contest here on the banks of the River Trent was inhospitable with the mercury peaking at 32 degrees.

articles.title: Tearful Bella Hadid sparks health fears as she reveals debilitating 'flare up' that's left her 'out of breath' and 'fainting'
articles.description: Bella Hadid sparked new health fears as she revealed a debilitating Lyme disease 'flare up' amid her years-long battle with the illness.

articles.title: Controversial billionaire tax will appear on November ballot
articles.description: Proponents of a controversial billionaire tax pledge to making sure it appears on the November ballot to backfill federal healthcare funds.

articles.title: Trump nominates Chris Klomp as HHS second-in-command
articles.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday named Medicare director and former health care executive Chris Klomp to be deputy secretary of the US Department of Health and Human Services.

articles.title: Ashley Tisdale's Go-To Face Cream Is 'Absolute Magic' for Sensitive Skin
articles.description: Few skincare concerns are as frustrating as dryness. Finding the right moisturizer can be tough, but Ashley Tisdale is letting fans in on the moisturizing cream she swears by. The Illiyoon Ceramide Ato Concentrate Cream is a multi-use formula designed for dry, sensitive skin and those with a compromised skin barrier. Tisdale praised the formula [...]

articles.title: Trump nominates new top deputy to RFK Jr.
articles.description: Chris Klomp, a 45-year-old tech entrepreneur, gained the president's confidence when he negotiated price cuts with drug companies.

articles.title: June brings on ALS Awareness Month
articles.description: Doctors say awareness is crucial, especially for early diagnosis and access to care.

articles.title: Serious statin side effects on muscles are extremely rare, new research confirms
articles.description: People are more worried about severe muscular problems when taking statins than they should be: These side effects are exceedingly rare, research published Thursday in the journal The Lancet Digital Health reaffirms.

articles.title: ‘Not acceptable’: Ontario pharmacist sounds alarm on drug shortages
articles.description: It has been a frustrating few weeks for Kyro Maseh behind the counter of his Beaches pharmacy. Patients are showing up with prescriptions for common drugs to treat glaucoma, post-surgery pain, and heart burn. Those medications are backordered and in many cases the alternatives are hard to come by with no clear answers about when stock will return to normal.

articles.title: Opinion: Red light therapy didn’t go viral by accident
articles.description: The wellness craze may overpromise, but its popularity reveals how badly people want relief from burnout.

articles.title: US Supreme Court sides with Bayer, blocking thousands of Roundup cancer lawsuits
articles.description: The US Supreme Court on Thursday sided with Bayer in a ruling expected to block thousands of lawsuits claiming its Roundup weedkiller caused cancer by failing to carry adequate warning labels. The decision hands a victory to President Donald Trump's administration but drew backlash from allies in the "Make America Healthy Again" movement.

articles.title: This autism therapy is covered by many insurers. Why does the military’s plan restrict it?
articles.description: TRICARE, the military’s healthcare program, limits coverage of applied behavior analysis, a type of therapy for people with autism. Kristi Cabiao is trying to get the policy changed.

articles.description: Merrimack Health Methuen Hospital's birthing center will close its doors on June 29 and move patients to Merrimack Health Lawrence Hospital.

articles.description: At a time when energy security and affordability have risen to the top of the development agenda, 655 million people globally still lack access to electricity, and two billion use polluting fuels and technologies for cooking putting their health and well-being at risk.

articles.description: I'm pregnant - and have restless legs syndrome (RLS), a common disorder described by the NHS as an overwhelming urge to move your legs to stop an uncomfortable sensation.
